items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of the city's key commercial corridors. This represents significant economic activity and potential job creation in the area.

items.headline: Stadium Neighborhood Relations committee continues addressing stadium issues
items.summary: The ad hoc subcommittee overseeing relations between the city and residents near Levi's Stadium is meeting to discuss neighborhood concerns. This committee handles complaints and coordination related to the stadium's impact on local residents.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city council approved outreach efforts for a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers, likely aimed at bringing unlicensed businesses into compliance. This could affect local business regulations and shopping center operations across Cupertino.

items.headline: City council introduces economic incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: New economic development incentives for retail businesses, small businesses, and startups were discussed as part of broader business support initiatives. This represents an effort to attract and retain commercial activity in Cupertino.

items.headline: Community development grant funding applications presented to commission
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for federal HOME and CDBG funding on March 25, which support affordable housing and community development projects. These grants can bring significant resources to address local housing and service needs.

items.headline: Planning Commission to Study San Antonio Road Area Transportation and Land Use
items.summary: The commission will undertake a study session on potential land use and transportation changes for the San Antonio Road area. This could affect how the corridor develops and how residents move through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: New Retail Vitality Ordinance Aims to Strengthen Commercial Districts
items.summary: The commission is considering amendments to zoning and building codes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. The changes are intended to support and revitalize commercial areas across Palo Alto.

items.headline: City approves power grid easement across wastewater facility
items.summary: San José approved an easement agreement allowing LS Power Grid California to install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project will affect a major city facility and may have implications for future utility upgrades in the region.
